q.en: When and where was Mohandas Gandhi born?
q.zh: 莫罕达斯·甘地(Mohandas Gandhi)是在何时、何地出生的？

options.en: On October 2, 1869, into a large Hindu family in India
options.zh: 1869年10月2日，出生在印度一个虔诚的印度教大家庭

options.en: In 1869 in England
options.zh: 1869年，在英国

options.en: In 1947 in Pakistan
options.zh: 1947年，在巴基斯坦

options.en: In 1888 in South Africa according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 1888年，在南非（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, 'Gandhi was born into a large Hindu family on October 2, 1869.' He was later called the 'Father of India.'
explain.zh: 书里说：「甘地于1869年10月2日出生在一个庞大的印度教家庭。」他后来被称为「印度之父」。

q.en: In South Africa, why was Gandhi thrown off a train?
q.zh: 在南非时，甘地为什么被赶下火车？

options.en: Because the train was full according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 因为火车已经满员（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: Because he was late
options.zh: 因为他迟到了

options.en: Because, although he had a first-class ticket, he was told he could not sit in the first-class car due to the color of his skin
options.zh: 因为尽管他买了头等车厢的票，却因为是他的肤色，被命令不能坐头等车厢

options.en: Because he had no ticket
options.zh: 因为他没有买票

explain.en: The book says a conductor told him he couldn't sit in the first-class car even though he had bought a first-class ticket; 'None of that mattered because of the color of Gandhi's skin. When Gandhi would not leave, he was thrown off the train.'
explain.zh: 书里说，尽管甘地买了头等车厢的票，列车员仍不让他坐；「这些都不管用，因为甘地的肤色。甘地不肯离开，就被赶下了火车。」

q.en: What happened during the Salt March of 1930?
q.zh: 1930年的「食盐长征」(Salt March) 发生了什么？

options.en: He fasted for peace in Calcutta according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他在加尔各答绝食以祈求和平（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: He sailed to England to study law
options.zh: 他乘船去英国学法律

options.en: He was elected president of India
options.zh: 他当选印度总统

options.en: Gandhi led a 21-day, 240-mile walk to the sea, picked up a grain of salt, and was arrested with more than 60,000 others
options.zh: 甘地带领大家进行了21天、240英里（约386公里）的步行直达海边，他捡起一粒盐，并与六万多人一起被捕

explain.en: The book says, 'On March 12, Gandhi and some followers began a 21-day, 240-mile walk across India to the sea... He picked up a grain of salt... Gandhi was arrested, along with more than 60,000 others.'
explain.zh: 书里说：「3月12日，甘地和一些追随者开始了21天、240英里横跨印度走向大海的步行……他捡起一粒盐……甘地和六万多人一起被捕。」

options.en: He died in a train accident in South Africa according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他在南非死于火车事故（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: On January 30, 1948, a young Hindu man who disapproved of his work for peace with Muslims shot him three times
options.zh: 1948年1月30日，一名不赞同他与穆斯林寻求和平的年轻印度教徒向他开了三枪

options.en: He was killed during the Salt March
options.zh: 他在食盐长征中被杀

options.en: He died of old age in 1869
options.zh: 他于1869年自然老死

explain.en: The book says, 'On January 30, 1948... A young Hindu man in the crowd didn't approve of Gandhi's work to make peace with Muslims. He shot Gandhi three times.'
explain.zh: 书里说：「1948年1月30日……人群里一名年轻的印度教徒不赞同甘地与穆斯林谋求和平的工作。他向甘地开了三枪。」

q.zh: 甘地的词「satyagraha」(坚持真理) 是什么意思？

options.en: 'Fighting with weapons'
options.zh: 「用武器战斗」

options.en: 'Holding firmly to the truth,' which he used to represent nonviolent protest
options.zh: 「紧紧抓住真理」，他用它来代表非暴力的抗议

options.en: 'Ruling a country'
options.zh: 「统治一个国家」

options.en: 'Studying law in England' according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 「在英国学法律」（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book explains, 'satyagraha (suh-TYAH-gruh-huh). The word means "holding firmly to the truth." Gandhi used it to represent his form of nonviolent protest.'
explain.zh: 书里解释：「satyagraha。这个词的意思是『紧紧抓住真理』。甘地用它来代表自己的非暴力抗议形式。」

q.zh: 在词汇表里，「非暴力」(nonviolence) 是什么？

options.en: A type of government
options.zh: 一种政府形式

options.en: The refusal to use physical force or violence as a response
options.zh: 拒绝以体力或暴力作为回应

options.en: A religious festival
options.zh: 一个宗教节日

options.en: A way to travel by train according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种乘火车出行的方式（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines nonviolence as 'the refusal to use physical force or violence as a response (p. 5).' It was a core Hindu belief Gandhi lived by.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 nonviolence 定义为「拒绝以体力或暴力作为回应」（第5页）。这是甘地一生奉行的印度教核心信念。

q.en: The book says Gandhi's strong beliefs included fairness, freedom, kindness, and peace. How did these beliefs affect the world?
q.zh: 书里说甘地坚定的信念包括公平、自由、善良与和平。这些信念对世界产生了什么影响？

options.en: They had no effect on anyone
options.zh: 它们对任何人都没有影响

options.en: They made him a wealthy businessman
options.zh: 这些信念让他成了富有的商人

options.en: They caused him to leave India forever according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 这些信念让他永远离开了印度（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: His beliefs in nonviolence and peace changed nations and continue to inspire people
options.zh: 他关于非暴力与和平的信念改变了许多国家，并持续激励着人们

explain.en: The book says of the quiet man, 'His strong beliefs in fairness, freedom, kindness, and peace changed nations,' and 'continues to inspire people around the world.'
explain.zh: 书里说这位安静的男人「对公平、自由、善良与和平的坚定信念改变了许多国家」，并且「持续激励着全世界的人们」。

q.en: How did Gandhi's example spread beyond India?
q.zh: 甘地的榜样是怎样传播到印度以外的？

options.en: He traveled to every country as its president according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他以总统身份访问了每一个国家（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: He invented a new religion
options.zh: 他创立了一种新宗教

options.en: News of his nonviolent protests reached around the world, and he later inspired leaders like Martin Luther King Jr. and Nelson Mandela
options.zh: 他非暴力抗议的消息传遍全球，后来还激励了马丁·路德·金和纳尔逊·曼德拉等领袖

options.en: He wrote a best-selling novel
options.zh: 他写了一本畅销小说

explain.en: The book notes that news of the protests 'reached around the world,' and in its legacy section says he 'inspired such great leaders as Martin Luther King Jr. and Nelson Mandela.'
explain.zh: 书里提到抗议的消息「传遍了全世界」；在「遗产」一节中说他「激励了马丁·路德·金和纳尔逊·曼德拉等伟大领袖」。

q.en: The author gives Gandhi the title 'Father of India' and the name 'Mahatma,' meaning 'Great Soul.' What is the author's main purpose?
q.zh: 作者给甘地「印度之父」的称号，又称他「Mahatma」（伟大的灵魂）。作者的主要意图是什么？

options.en: To show admiration and inspire readers with Gandhi's peaceful, influential life
options.zh: 为了表达敬仰，并用甘地和平而有影响力的一生来激励读者

options.en: To explain how to make salt
options.zh: 为了讲解怎样制盐

options.en: To prove Gandhi was the richest man in India according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了证明甘地是印度最有钱的人（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To argue that Gandhi was a president
options.zh: 为了论证甘地是一位总统

explain.en: The book calls him 'The Father of India' who 'continues to inspire people around the world,' and says Indians called him Mahatma, or 'Great Soul' — the author wants readers to admire and learn from him.
explain.zh: 书里称他为「印度之父」，「持续激励着全世界的人们」，并说印度人称他 Mahatma，即「伟大的灵魂」——作者是想让读者敬仰他、向他学习。

q.en: Instead of using violence, the book focuses on Gandhi's peaceful methods like satyagraha, the Salt March, and fasting. Why?
q.zh: 书里没有写暴力革命，而是聚焦在甘地的和平方式上，比如坚持真理、食盐长征和绝食。为什么要这样写？

options.en: To show that nonviolent protest and peaceful action can change nations and win justice
options.zh: 为了展现非暴力的抗议与和平的行动也能改变国家、争取到公正

options.en: To make the story shorter
options.zh: 为了让故事更短

options.en: Because Gandhi did not know how to fight according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为甘地不懂得怎样打架（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because violence was illegal in India
options.zh: 因为在印度暴力是违法的

explain.en: The whole book centers on Gandhi's belief in nonviolence — from satyagraha to the Salt March to fasting for peace — to show readers that peaceful methods can achieve great change.
explain.zh: 整本书都围绕甘地的非暴力信念展开——从坚持真理到食盐长征，再到为和平绝食——向读者说明：和平的方式也能带来巨大的改变。

prompt.en: Gandhi believed in satyagraha — 'holding firmly to the truth' — and used nonviolent protest (the Salt March, fasting, peaceful marches) instead of fighting back with violence. Think of a time you solved a disagreement or stood up for something without using force or meanness. What happened, and what can we learn from Gandhi's belief that peaceful action can be stronger than violence?
prompt.zh: 甘地相信「satyagraha」——「紧紧抓住真理」，并用非暴力的方式（食盐长征、绝食、和平游行）来代替以暴制暴。回想一次你不用武力或恶意，就解决了争执或坚持了某件事的经历。当时发生了什么？从甘地「和平行动可能比暴力更有力量」的信念里，我们能学到什么？

en: Gandhi's satyagraha meant 'holding firmly to the truth' and stood for nonviolent protest (p. 8).
In the Salt March of 1930 he walked 240 miles to the sea and picked up salt to protest an unfair tax (p. 11-12).
He also used fasting as a form of protest to bring peace (p. 14).
A good answer gives a real personal example of solving conflict peacefully and connects it to Gandhi's idea.
zh: 甘地的 satyagraha 意为「紧紧抓住真理」，代表非暴力抗议（第8页）。
1930年的食盐长征中，他步行240英里到海边捡盐，抗议不公平的盐税（第11-12页）。
他还把绝食当作一种抗议方式来促成和平（第14页）。
好的回答要举一个真实、和平解决冲突的个人例子，并把它和甘地的理念联系起来。
